name: Welsh Cheese and Leek Breakfast Bake
steps: Soften the leeks in the butter, 6-7 minutes.
Beat the eggs, pour over the leeks, scatter with the cheese, and cook until just set.
steps_detailed: Soften the leeks in the butter over medium-low heat, 8-9 minutes, until fully tender.
Beat the eggs with the pepper, pour over the leeks, scatter with the cheese, and cook gently, lifting the edges occasionally, until just set.

name: Welsh Cakes
steps: Rub the butter into the flour and sugar until crumbly.
Stir in the currants and enough egg and milk to bring it together into a dough.
Roll out, cut into rounds, and cook on a dry griddle 2-3 minutes per side.
steps_detailed: Rub the butter into the flour, sugar, and cinnamon until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in the currants and enough beaten egg to bring it together into a firm dough.
Roll out to about a quarter-inch thick, cut into rounds, and cook on a dry, hot griddle, 2-3 minutes per side, until lightly speckled and cooked through.
Welsh cakes are cooked on a griddle rather than baked, a distinction that goes back to households without a proper oven.

name: Welsh Oat Porridge with Honey
steps: Simmer the oats in the milk for 6-8 minutes, stirring, until thick.
Top with the honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the oats in the milk and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring often, 8-10 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Top generously with the honey before serving.

name: Welsh Cawl
steps: Brown the lamb.
Add the leeks, potato, cabbage, and 2 cups water, and simmer 25-30 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the onion and pepper over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the leeks, potato, cabbage, and 2.25 cups water, and simmer 28-32 minutes until all the vegetables are fully tender.
Cawl, built around lamb and leeks, is considered the closest thing Wales has to a national dish, traditionally simmered a day ahead so the flavors settle.

name: Welsh Rarebit
steps: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook 1 minute, then add the milk gradually, followed by the cheese and pepper, stirring until smooth, thick, and glossy.
Toast the bread, spoon the cheese mixture generously over the top, and grill or broil 2-3 minutes until bubbling and lightly browned.
Rarebit is a proper cooked cheese sauce, not simply melted cheese on toast - the distinction matters to anyone who grew up on the real thing.
steps_detailed: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook 1 minute, then add the milk gradually, followed by the cheese and pepper, stirring until smooth, thick, and glossy.
Toast the bread, spoon the cheese mixture generously over the top, and grill or broil 2-3 minutes until bubbling and lightly browned.
Rarebit is a proper cooked cheese sauce, not simply melted cheese on toast - the distinction matters to anyone who grew up on the real thing.

Real sliced bread, toasted, is used here now rather than a tortilla standing in for it - a tortilla's texture is nothing like toast, while a genuine sliced loaf toasts up properly crisp the way the dish actually calls for.

name: Welsh Leek and Potato Soup
steps: Soften the leeks in the butter, 8-10 minutes.
Add the potato and 1.5 cups water, and simmer 18-20 minutes until tender.
Stir in the milk and warm through.
steps_detailed: Soften the leeks in the butter over medium-low heat, 10-12 minutes, until fully tender.
Add the potato, pepper, and 1.75 cups water, and simmer 20-22 minutes until the potato is very tender, mashing some of it against the pot to thicken slightly.
Stir in the milk and warm through gently without boiling - a simple, quiet soup built entirely around the leek's sweetness.

name: Welsh Glamorgan-Style Cheese and Leek Patties
steps: Soften the leeks briefly, then mix with the cheese, flour, and egg into a thick mixture.
Form into patties and fry in oil, 3-4 minutes per side, until golden.
steps_detailed: Soften the leeks in a dry pan for 3-4 minutes to take the raw edge off, then mix with the cheese, flour, egg, and pepper into a thick, cohesive mixture.
Form into small patties and fry in the oil over medium heat, 3-4 minutes per side, until deeply golden and cooked through.
Traditionally built around leeks in place of meat, these patties have long served as a vegetarian option well before the term was common.

name: Welsh Lamb and Leek Pie
steps: Brown the lamb with the leeks.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, and cold water, line a small dish, fill with the lamb mixture, top with more pastry, and bake at 400F for 20-25 minutes.
steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the leeks and potato over medium-high heat, 10-12 minutes.
Rub the butter into the flour with a pinch of salt and enough cold water to form a firm pastry.
Line a small dish with half the pastry, fill with the lamb mixture, top with the remaining pastry, seal, and bake at 400F (200C) for 22-26 minutes until golden.

name: Welsh Cottage Pie with Leeks
steps: Brown the beef with the leeks and onion.
Spread the mashed potato over the top and bake at 400F for 15-20 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the leeks and onion over medium-high heat, 10-12 minutes.
Mash the potato with the butter, spread over the beef mixture, and bake at 400F (200C) for 18-22 minutes until golden.
Making leeks rather than plain onion the backbone of the base is what turns an ordinary cottage pie distinctly Welsh.

name: Welsh Baked Fish with Leeks
steps: Soften the leeks in the butter, 8 minutes.
Lay the fish over the leeks, cover, and bake at 375F for 15-18 minutes until cooked through. Finish with the lime juice.
steps_detailed: Soften the leeks in the butter over medium-low heat, 9-10 minutes, until tender.
Season the fish with pepper, lay it over the leeks, cover, and bake at 375F (190C) for 16-20 minutes until just cooked through and flaking.
Finish with the lime juice before serving.

name: Welsh Braised Cabbage and Lamb
steps: Brown the lamb with the onion in the butter.
Add the cabbage and a splash of water, cover, and simmer 15-18 minutes until tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the onion and pepper in the butter over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the cabbage and a generous splash of water, cover, and simmer 18-20 minutes until the cabbage is fully tender and the flavors have melded.

name: Welsh Bara Brith
steps: Soak the currants in warm water for 20 minutes, then drain.
Mix the flour, baking powder, honey, and currants with enough egg and milk to form a thick batter, and bake at 350F for 30-35 minutes.
steps_detailed: Soak the currants in warm water for 25-30 minutes to plump them, then drain well.
Mix the flour, baking powder, cinnamon, honey, egg, and drained currants with a splash of milk into a thick, spoonable batter.
Pour into a small lined pan and bake at 350F (175C) for 35-40 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
Bara brith is traditionally soaked in strong tea overnight - soaking the currants in warm water instead keeps the same soft, plump texture.

name: Welsh Cheese and Leek Scones
steps: Soften the leeks briefly.
Mix the flour, baking powder, cheese, and leeks with butter and milk into a soft dough, pat into rounds, and bake at 400F for 12-15 minutes.
steps_detailed: Soften the leeks in a dry pan for 3-4 minutes to take the raw edge off.
Rub the butter into the flour and baking powder until crumbly, then stir in most of the cheese, the leeks, and enough milk to form a soft dough.
Pat into rounds, top with the remaining cheese, and bake at 400F (200C) for 13-16 minutes until risen and golden.

name: Welsh Cabbage Slaw with Honey Dressing
steps: Whisk the honey, lime juice, and oil together.
Toss with the shredded cabbage and let sit 10 minutes before serving.
steps_detailed: Whisk the honey, lime juice, oil, and pepper together into a light dressing.
Toss with the shredded cabbage and let sit 12-15 minutes so it softens slightly before serving.

name: Welsh Herring on Rye

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the rye bread and top with the pickled herring.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the rye bread generously and top with the pickled herring, finishing with a little black pepper - a simple open-faced snack drawing on the same fish-and-bread pairing found right along the Atlantic coast.

name: Welsh Honey Cake
steps: Cream the butter and honey, beat in the egg, then fold in the flour.
Bake at 350F for 20-22 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and honey together until light, then beat in the egg.
Fold in the flour, cinnamon, and baking powder, pour into a small lined pan, and bake at 350F (175C) for 22-26 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.

name: Welsh Rice Pudding with Currants
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the sugar for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
Stir in the currants in the last few minutes.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the sugar and cinnamon over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Stir in the currants for the last 5 minutes so they plump slightly, and serve warm.

name: Welsh Rye Bread and Butter Pudding

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the torn bread and layer with the currants in a small dish.
Pour the milk over, let it soak 10 minutes, and bake at 350F for 20-25 minutes until set.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the torn rye bread generously and layer with the currants in a small baking dish.
Whisk the milk, egg, and sugar together and pour over, letting it soak 12-15 minutes.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 22-28 minutes until set and golden - the rye gives this version a deeper, slightly savory edge against the sweetness.

name: Welsh Cheese Sauce
steps: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, then add the milk gradually.
Stir in the cheese until melted and smooth.
steps_detailed: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook 1 minute, then whisk in the milk gradually, simmering until thickened.
Stir in the cheese and pepper until fully melted and smooth - looser and simpler than a full rarebit, meant for pouring over vegetables or potatoes.

name: Welsh Honey and Leek Glaze
steps: Soften the minced leek in the butter until very tender.
Stir in the honey and simmer briefly into a light glaze.
steps_detailed: Soften the minced leek in the butter over low heat, 6-7 minutes, until completely tender and lightly caramelized at the edges.
Stir in the honey and pepper and simmer 2-3 minutes into a light, spoonable glaze - good brushed over roasted meat or vegetables.
